title: narcissus asturiensis subsp. jacetanus
intro: Narcissus asturiensis subsp. jacetanus, also known as the Jacetan Daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white or yellow trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the nervous system and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Narcissus asturiensis subsp. jacetanus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of depression, anxiety, and insomnia due to its adaptogenic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the flowers and bulbs, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
